MLB PRO REPORT | 2026-04-16

HEADLINE
Major League Baseball's schedule is driving the day, with the board centered on matchup setup, probable starters, and game-state movement.

SNAPSHOT
The MLB slate currently shows 10 final game(s).

KEY DATA POINTS
- The MLB board shows 10 total game(s) in this report window.
- The board is being driven more by game state and results than by pregame probable-starter context.
- Foster Griffin is set to start for Washington Nationals versus Pittsburgh Pirates at 12:35 PM ET, and his recent Statcast sample covers 340 pitches, giving this matchup a measurable pitching signal to watch.
- Braxton Ashcraft gets the ball for Pittsburgh Pirates against Washington Nationals at 12:35 PM ET and has touched 99.1 mph; has carried an average spin rate around 2497, making him one of the stronger Statcast pitching signals on tonight's board.

WHY IT MATTERS
- Limited probable-starter detail on parts of the board increases the value of matchup framing and schedule context.
- With finals already posting, the board supports both quick-turn recap writing and forward-looking setup for the remaining slate.

STORY ANGLES

- Washington Nationals at Pittsburgh Pirates (12:35 PM ET) features a listed starters matchup of Foster Griffin vs. Braxton Ashcraft, giving the game a strong pregame pitching frame.
- San Francisco Giants at Cincinnati Reds (12:40 PM ET) draws attention with Landen Roupp lined up against Chase Burns, making starting pitching the clearest early storyline.
